question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `data` field containing a crafted JSON structure that causes the DATA_PATTERN regex in LoggingFilter.reorderFields() to exhibit catastrophic backtracking, consuming excessive CPU time proportional to the payload size and causing a denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `to` address pointing to a precompile contract that performs expensive cryptographic operations, causing each request to consume close to requestTimeout seconds, and by sending requests at the maximum requestsPerSecond rate, saturate all available processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `data` field that is valid hex but decodes to a byte sequence that causes the EVM to throw an IllegalStateException, which is caught and rethrown as MirrorEvmTransactionException, causing LoggingFilter.getMessage() to call getFullMessage() and expose internal EVM state details in the response that reveal contract internals?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `block` parameter set to a block number that requires loading a very large historical state snapshot, causing the database query to run for the full requestTimeout duration, and by sending many such requests simultaneously, exhaust the database statement timeout (3000ms) and cause cascading failures across all web3 API endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `data` field containing a crafted hex string that, when processed by LoggingFilter.getContent(), causes the GZIP compression to produce output larger than the input (incompressible data), bypassing the compression optimization and falling through to the truncation path, but the truncation cuts the hex string at an odd position, causing the logged data to be invalid hex that misrepresents the actual transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with `estimate=true` and a `gas` value of exactly 21_000 (minimum allowed by @Min(21_000)) that causes the gas estimation to return an estimate lower than the actual gas required, and then submit the actual transaction with that underestimated gas, causing it to fail with OUT_OF_GAS while the attacker knew in advance it would fail?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `data` field containing a crafted payload that, after StringUtils.deleteWhitespace() processing, produces a string that is exactly maxPayloadLogSize characters long but represents a different transaction than the original, allowing the attacker to make the logs show a benign transaction while the actual executed transaction was malicious?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `to` address of Address.ZERO (empty `to` field) and a large `data` field containing contract creation bytecode, causing the EVM to attempt contract deployment that consumes the full requestTimeout, and by doing so repeatedly, prevent legitimate contract deployment simulations from completing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `data` field that is a valid hex string of length exactly maxPayloadLogSize*10 bytes (the ContentCachingRequestWrapper buffer limit), causing the wrapper to buffer exactly that amount, and then submit a follow-up byte that is silently dropped, causing the EVM to execute a different function selector than intended without any error indication?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `gasPrice` of Long.MAX_VALUE (maximum long value) that causes an arithmetic overflow in the gas cost calculation within the EVM, resulting in a negative gas cost that bypasses the gas throttle check and allows the transaction to execute without consuming any throttle tokens?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `data` field containing a crafted hex string that, when decoded and executed by the EVM, causes a MirrorEvmTransactionException with a very long error message, which is then logged by LoggingFilter.getMessage() without any size limit, causing unbounded log entries regardless of maxPayloadLogSize?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `value` of Long.MAX_VALUE (maximum long, passes @PositiveOrZero) that causes an arithmetic overflow in the EVM's value transfer calculation, resulting in a negative transfer amount that incorrectly passes balance checks and executes a value transfer that should have been rejected?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `data` field containing a crafted hex string that causes the reorderFields() regex in LoggingFilter to match and move the `data` field to the end of the log entry, but the moved content contains a crafted string that, when appended, causes the log parser to misinterpret subsequent log entries as part of this transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `block` parameter set to a block number that is just below the current block, causing the EVM to load state from a block that was recently pruned from the database, triggering a BlockNumberNotFoundException that is thrown after the gas throttle bucket has been debited but before gas restoration occurs?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `data` field containing a crafted hex string that, when processed by the EVM, causes a contract to emit a very large event log, which is then included in the MirrorEvmTransactionException error message and logged by LoggingFilter without size limits, causing log flooding?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with `estimate=true` and a `to` address pointing to a contract that always reverts, causing the gas estimation binary search to run all maxGasEstimateRetriesCount=20 iterations (each a full EVM execution), and by sending many such requests simultaneously, exhaust server resources before requestTimeout fires?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `data` field containing a crafted hex string that, when decoded by hexToBytes() in ContractController, produces a byte array with a specific function selector that triggers a precompile contract to return a very large output, which is then included in the ContractCallResponse and logged, bypassing the maxPayloadLogSize limit on the response side?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `from` address that is a valid hex address but does not exist in the mirror node database, causing the EVM to perform multiple database lookups that each approach the db.statementTimeout (3000ms), and by sending many such requests, exhaust the database connection pool before requestTimeout fires?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `data` field containing a crafted hex string that, after GZIP compression in LoggingFilter.getContent(), produces a Base64-encoded string that contains characters matching the DATA_PATTERN regex, causing reorderFields() to corrupt the compressed log entry and making it impossible to decompress and analyze the original transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `gas` value of exactly evmProperties.getMaxGasLimit() (15_000_000) and a `data` field that causes the EVM to perform exactly that many gas units of computation, consuming the full requestTimeout and causing the throttle bucket to be drained by the maximum possible amount per request?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `data` field containing a crafted hex string that causes the EVM to throw an IllegalArgumentException with a message containing sensitive internal state information (contract addresses, storage slots), which is then caught by ContractCallService.doProcessCall() and rethrown as MirrorEvmTransactionException, exposing this information in the HTTP response?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `data` field containing a crafted hex string that, when processed by LoggingFilter, causes the ByteArrayOutputStream to be initialized with a negative size (content.length()/4 when content.length() < 4), throwing a NegativeArraySizeException that is silently caught, causing the full uncompressed payload to be logged regardless of maxPayloadLogSize?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `data` field containing a crafted hex string that causes the EVM to produce a revert reason that is exactly maxPayloadLogSize characters long, causing the full revert reason to be logged without truncation (since it fits within the limit), but the revert reason contains sensitive information about the contract's internal state?
